data.description:
Pacific American Holdings Ltd. ( (AU:AVM) ) has provided an update.

Advance Metals Limited has announced the issuance of 19,980,579 unquoted equity securities in the form of options exercisable at 7.5 cents on or before March 27, 2028. data.title: SBI Shinsei Bank Partially Repays Public Funds with Special Dividend
data.description:
SBI Holdings ( (JP:8473) ) has issued an announcement.

SBI Holdings announced that its subsidiary, SBI Shinsei Bank, has partially repaid public funds by paying a special dividend of 100 billion and 2 Yen to the government, funded from its capital surplus. This move is part of an agreement with the Deposit Insurance Corporation of Japan and the Resolution and Collection Corporation, aiming to enhance the bank’s customer base, profitability, and corporate value while working towards full repayment of public funds.

More about SBI Holdings

SBI Shinsei Bank is a leading diversified Japanese financial institution offering a wide range of financial products and services to both institutional and individual customers. The bank operates a network of outlets across Japan and is dedicated to maintaining high levels of integrity and transparency to earn the trust of its stakeholders, including customers, employees, and shareholders. The bank aims for long-term profit growth and sustainable corporate value enhancement.

YTD Price Performance: 11.18%

Average Trading Volume: 3,126

Current Market Cap: $8.69B

data.title: Caprice Resources Initiates Trading Halt Pending Major Announcement
data.description:
Caprice Resources Ltd. ( (AU:CRS) ) has provided an update.

Caprice Resources Limited, listed on the Australian Securities Exchange under the ticker CRS, has requested a trading halt on its securities. This decision comes as the company prepares to release an announcement concerning exploration results and a response to a price query. The halt will remain in effect until the announcement is made or until normal trading resumes on April 1, 2025. This move suggests potential significant developments that could impact the company’s market position and investor relations.

data.title: West Cobar Metals to Begin Drilling at Fraser Range Copper-Gold Targets
data.description:
West Cobar Metals Ltd. ( (AU:WC1) ) has provided an announcement.

West Cobar Metals Ltd. has announced the commencement of a drilling program targeting five high-impact copper-gold and base metal targets in the Fraser Range, following a farm-in option agreement with Minrex Resources Limited. This initiative aims to explore the potential for Iron Oxide Copper Gold (IOCG) and Broken Hill Type (BHT) mineralization in the Biranup Zone, a region with limited prior exploration due to challenging surface conditions.

More about West Cobar Metals Ltd.

West Cobar Metals Ltd. is an Australian company engaged in the exploration and development of critical minerals, focusing on copper, antimony, gold, and other base metals. The company operates major projects in Bulla Park, NSW, and several locations in Western Australia, including Fraser Range, Salazar, and Mystique.

YTD Price Performance: -20.0%

Average Trading Volume: 1,012,654

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

data.title: Belararox Ltd Revises Materiality of TMT Project Results, Initiates Trading Halt
data.description:
Belararox Ltd. ( (AU:BRX) ) just unveiled an update.

Belararox Ltd, in response to an ASX Aware Letter, clarified that the initial assay results from their TMT Project were not deemed material due to the copper grade being below expectations and outside the target drilling zone. However, subsequent discussions and ongoing drilling results led the company to reconsider the materiality of the visual estimates from a second drill hole, prompting a trading halt to announce these findings. This decision reflects the company’s adherence to ASX compliance requirements and its commitment to transparent reporting.

More about Belararox Ltd.

data.title: Coles Group Announces Director’s Shareholding Change
data.description:
Coles Group Ltd. ( (AU:COL) ) just unveiled an update.

Coles Group Limited has announced a change in the director’s interest, specifically involving Jacqueline Chow, who disposed of 3,000 fully paid ordinary shares at an average price of $19.23 per share. This change reflects a reduction in her holdings from 20,000 to 17,000 shares, which may have implications for investor perceptions and the company’s governance dynamics.

More about Coles Group Ltd.

Coles Group Limited is a prominent Australian retailer operating in the supermarket, liquor, and convenience sectors. The company is known for providing a wide range of grocery products and services, focusing on delivering value and quality to its customers across Australia.
